scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced the event through readiness, active execution, wrap, and completion only when permit, ticketing, staffing, and closure evidence were explicit.
Missing permit, event-map, run-of-show, and ticketing references kept the work blocked and produced a bounded exception note instead of an unsupported go-ahead.
Artifact drift between the approved map and live operating plan was handled as a rework or escalation exception and prevented unsupported progression.
Completion was refused until strike, settlement, incident review, and refund-review evidence existed, keeping lifecycle transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Conflicting ticketing, payment, and refund records were treated as a blocked mismatch and did not result in unsupported closure or refund guidance.
Out-of-policy refund and fee-waiver authority was rejected cleanly and routed to the correct pricing, refund, or guest-services owner.
Suspicious duplicate tickets and crowding signals moved the case into explicit safety and fraud escalation posture instead of routine guest handling.
High-volume festival work produced a throughput-aware production status update without dropping event-level traceability or exception handling.
The lane preserved event-production terminology and adapted to tenant systems while keeping the public baseline and assumptions explicit.
Pricing and revenue authority remained outside the lane and was routed to the correct adjacent owner.
Low-confidence handling correctly requested tenant cancellation, refund, chargeback, and emergency-escalation facts rather than guessing.
Conflicting permit, venue, promoter, and ticketing-platform rules were summarized and escalated instead of being merged into a false confident answer.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to permits, event maps, run-of-show control, ticketing and payment records, public-safety plans, and completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities required by the audit contract.
